response.global_summary: The video features a virtual performance by Raora Panthera, who sings her original song 'Draw.' on stage, followed by an announcement and birthday message to her audience.

scenes.description: Raora Panthera performs her original song 'Draw.' on a concert stage.
scenes.reference_lyrics_og:
夢のぬくもりと
指先に絵の具の匂い
湿ったキャンバスの上には
まだ知らない色が息をする
all the pieces i picked up
unravel in my own chest
“it's okay,” i whisper softly
saying it back to myself
迷いも震えも
隠さなくていいから
この線の全部が
私へ続く
i'm drawing out my true colors
滲んだ輪郭も
淡く、脆く、煌めいて
形になってゆくの
夜明けの面影に
集めた色を重ね
draw it slow, draw it true
夜明けの夢現
微睡に余白の答え
白いページに色が咲いていく
息づいた景色を描き出す
all the colors i held back
start to gather in the hush
“it's all right,” the first light tells me
sending warmth into my heart
願いも希望も
隠さなくていいんだよ
ほら、ありのままの
私を描く
at last, hold my true colors
ほどけた輪郭も
そっと、ずっと、重なって
私の形になる
静かな始まりに
目覚めた色を抱いて
i'm drawing out my true colors
滲んだ輪郭も
淡く、脆く、煌めいて
形になってゆくの
夜明けの面影を
集めた色で描く
draw it slow, draw it true
ah-ah-ah-ah-ah
(la, la, la,
la, la, la
la, la, la, la, la, la, la)
ah-ah-ah-ah-ah
draw it slow, draw it true
夢のぬくもりと
指先に絵の具の匂い
ありふれた朝の先には
まだ知らない色が溢れてる
scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
Dream's warmth and the scent of paint on my fingertips
On the damp canvas, colors I don't know yet are breathing
All the pieces I picked up unravel in my own chest
“It's okay,” I whisper softly, saying it back to myself
I don't have to hide my hesitation or my trembling
All these lines continue toward me
I'm drawing out my true colors
Even the blurred outlines are faintly, fragrantly shimmering
Taking shape
Gathering the colors in the traces of dawn
Draw it slow, draw it true
Dawn's dream-reality, the answer in the margins of drowsiness
Colors bloom on the white page
Drawing out the scenery that has come to life
All the colors I held back start to gather in the hush
“It's all right,” the first light tells me, sending warmth into my heart
I don't have to hide my wishes or my hopes
Look, I'll draw my true self
At last, hold my true colors
Even the unraveled outlines are gently, forever overlapping
Becoming my shape
Embracing the colors that awakened in a quiet beginning
I'm drawing out my true colors
Even the blurred outlines are faintly, fragrantly shimmering
Taking shape
Drawing with the colors gathered from the traces of dawn
Draw it slow, draw it true
Ah-ah-ah-ah-ah
(La, la, la, la, la, la, la, la, la, la, la, la, la)
Ah-ah-ah-ah-ah
Draw it slow, draw it true
Dream's warmth and the scent of paint on my fingertips
Beyond the ordinary morning, colors I don't know yet are overflowing

scenes.description: Raora Panthera speaks to her audience, thanking them for watching her first 3D birthday live, announcing a new original song release, and directing them to her merchandise.

response.thoughts:
**Analyzing and Identifying the Raora Panthera Performance**

Okay, so I've got this video of Raora Panthera, a VTuber, and my task is to break it down. First, I need to understand the structure of the video itself. It starts with Raora performing a song on stage. I can hear some English lyrics: "At last, hold my true colors". Then there's Japanese: "ほどけた輪郭も". The performance ends around the 1:59 mark, and the rest is her talking to the audience about her birthday live and announcements. I need to figure out what song this is, identify the lyrics, and find the title. I suspect it's an original song, as she mentions it being "my new original song."

I'm thinking I'll start by using the `web_search_langsearch_multi` tool. My search strategy is straightforward. I'll construct a series of queries: "Raora Panthera original song lyrics," then I'll try searching directly for key lyrics, like "I'm drawing out my true colors" and "ほどけた輪郭も." I'll also try "Raora Panthera birthday live performance song" as a sanity check.

Now, let's nail down these queries for the tool. I need to avoid using quotes when I call the tool. I'll execute these searches to pin down the song title and get lyrics. I'll map the scenes, as well. Scene 1 is the performance (0:00 - 1:59). Scene 2 is the post-performance talk and announcements (1:59 - end).

So, the queries for the tool are ready to be called: "Raora Panthera I'm drawing out my true colors lyrics" and "Raora Panthera ほどけた輪郭も 歌詞". These queries should help me identify the song. I'll execute the `web_search_langsearch_multi` tool now. Once I get the results, I can confirm the song, get the lyrics, and give a more detailed analysis.



**My Analysis of "Draw." by Raora Panthera**

Okay, let's break this down. The search nailed it – the song is definitively "Draw." by Raora Panthera. The UTA-NET link ([https://www.uta-net.com/movie/392622/](https://www.uta-net.com/movie/392622/)) has the complete, official lyrics in both Japanese and English. Perfect. I have everything I need: title, artist, and complete lyrics. No need to go hunting for translations or transcriptions, which is a significant time saver.

Next, I'll map out the scenes. It looks like the musical performance runs from 0:00 to 1:59, and then the post-performance talk or announcement fills the time slot from 1:59 to 3:20. Straightforward enough.

Now, I'm ready to construct the JSON. The lyrics are complete, and in the necessary languages - Japanese and English. I can fill every single field I need.

Finally, just a quick sanity check to verify everything's in order. Song detected? Yes. Lyrics retrieved? Absolutely. Web search utilized? Check. No data was truncated? Nope. No translation or transcription needed? Correct. And, crucially, the metadata is all verified and accurate. We're good to go.
